fib_s: docs/00-vision/REPORTING-LAYER/FIB-S-SHIFT-REPORT.json

      Define composite ShiftReportDTO (Pattern A, Contract-First) aggregating data

      downstream layers (WS2, WS3) are presentational only.

    estimated_complexity: high

    name: Report Page & Review Surface
    description: >
      RSC page at /admin/reports/shift-summary. Server component calls assembly

      Action toolbar sits outside the document area.

    estimated_complexity: high

  WS3:
    name: PDF Generation Route
    description: >
      POST /api/v1/reports/shift-summary/pdf route handler. Calls assembly service
      to construct ShiftReportDTO, renders via a JSX-based PDF template (9-section
      fixed layout), returns PDF binary with Content-Disposition header. Template is
      presentational only — no business logic. Preferred initial renderer:
      @react-pdf/renderer, subject to validation during WS3/WS5.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [CAP:generate_canonical_pdf, OUT-4, OUT-5, OUT-10, RULE-1, RULE-2, RULE-9, RULE-10, STEP-5]

    estimated_complexity: high

  WS4:
    name: Email Distribution Route
    description: >
      POST /api/v1/reports/shift-summary/send route handler. Assembles DTO,
      generates PDF buffer via the WS3 PDF renderer, sends as email attachment
      via EmailService.sendShiftReport(). Extends EmailProvider interface with
      optional attachments field (backward-compatible cross-context change).
      Idempotency must reuse existing email send-attempt tracking
      (services/email/crud.ts → email_send_attempt table, verified at
      services/email/index.ts:44). ServiceHttpResult<EmailSendResult> response.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [CAP:send_report_email, OUT-7, RULE-8, STEP-7, STEP-8]
    depends_on: [WS1, WS3]

      - lib/email/types.ts
      - lib/email/resend-adapter.ts
      - services/email/dtos.ts
      - services/email/index.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS5:
    name: Integration Tests & Quality Gates
    description: >
      Assembly service unit tests (DTO composition, totals reconciliation,
      parallel fetch error handling). Route handler HTTP contract tests for
      PDF and send endpoints. Final quality gate validation.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [infrastructure]
    depends_on: [WS1, WS3, WS4]
    outputs:
      - services/reporting/shift-report/__tests__/assembler.test.ts
      - services/reporting/shift-report/__tests__/http-contract.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS_E2E:
    name: E2E Write-Path Tests
    description: >
      Playwright specs covering the email send write-path user journey.
      WS4 delegates writes to EmailService's existing email_send_attempt
      persistence (verified: services/email/index.ts:44, crud.insertSendAttempt).
      E2E mandate applies per write-path classification. Tests the full flow:
      navigate to report page, select date/shift, generate report, send to
      recipients.
    executor: e2e-testing
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [infrastructure]
    depends_on: [WS1, WS2, WS3, WS4, WS5]
    outputs:
      - e2e/reporting/*.spec.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

execution_phases:
  - name: "Phase 1 — Foundation (DTO & Assembly)"
    parallel: [WS1]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 2 — Surfaces (Page + PDF, parallel)"
    parallel: [WS2, WS3]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 3 — Distribution"
    parallel: [WS4]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 4 — Quality"
    parallel: [WS5]
    gates: [test-pass]

  - name: "Phase 5 — E2E Validation"
    parallel: [WS_E2E]
    gates: [test-pass]

gates:
  type-check:
    command: "npm run type-check"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"

  test-pass:
    command: "npm test services/reporting/ > /tmp/test-output.log 2>&1"
    success_criteria: "All tests pass"

  build:
    command: "npm run build > /tmp/build-output.log 2>&1"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no build errors"

  e2e-write-path:
    command: "npx playwright test e2e/reporting/ --reporter=list > /tmp/e2e-output.log 2>&1"
    success_criteria: "At least 1 spec exists, all tests pass"

external_dependencies:
  - service: TableContextService
    required_for: "ShiftDashboardSummaryDTO, CashObsTableRollupDTO (Section 1, 5)"
  - service: ShiftIntelligenceService
    required_for: "AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO, AlertQualityDTO (Section 4)"
  - service: MTLService
    required_for: "MtlGamingDaySummaryDTO (Section 3)"
  - service: MeasurementService
    required_for: "RatingCoverage, AuditCorrelation, LoyaltyLiability, TheoDiscrepancy (Sections 2, 5, 6)"
  - service: EmailService
    required_for: "Report email distribution with PDF attachment (WS4)"
  - service: CasinoService
    required_for: "Gaming day computation, shift boundary configuration"

decisions:
  - decision_id: DEC-001
    resolves_open_question: "Cash observation data: inline in financial summary table or separate sub-section?"
    decision_statement: "Cash observation data is inlined in the financial summary table as supplementary per-table columns alongside inventory-based metrics."
    rationale: "ShiftTableMetricsDTO already coexists with cash obs endpoints. Existing BFF pattern (getShiftDashboardSummary + getShiftCashObsTable) returns per-table data that naturally merges. Separate sub-section would fragment the financial view with no user benefit. Verified: services/table-context/shift-cash-obs.ts exports getShiftCashObsTable()."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

  - decision_id: DEC-002
    resolves_open_question: "Theo discrepancy: include as quality badge in Section 2 or defer?"
    decision_statement: "Theo discrepancy is included as a quality badge in Section 2 (Rating / Coverage Quality), showing computed vs legacy divergence summary."
    rationale: "queryTheoDiscrepancy() at services/measurement/queries.ts:92 already returns { rows: { id, table_id, computed_theo_cents, legacy_theo_cents }[] }. Assembly service computes aggregate divergence from these rows. No new data path needed. Verified: function exists with test coverage at services/measurement/__tests__/queries.test.ts:69."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

  - decision_id: DEC-003
    resolves_open_question: "PDF library selection"
    decision_statement: "@react-pdf/renderer is the preferred initial implementation path for canonical PDF generation, subject to validation during WS3/WS5. If it fails layout, compatibility, or operational requirements, substitute implementation is allowed without reopening feature scope, provided the canonical-artifact constraints (RULE-1, RULE-2, RULE-10) remain unchanged."
    rationale: "JSX-based templates match team React expertise. Runs natively in Node.js on Vercel Functions (Fluid Compute). Built-in pagination control, headers/footers. ~2MB bundle (acceptable server-side). No headless browser needed (puppeteer rejected: ~300MB Chromium, deployment complexity). jsPDF rejected: low-level API, difficult structured documents. This is an execution direction, not a frozen architecture — the FIB only established that the report must yield a canonical artifact via server-side generation."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: assumption

risks:
  - risk: "FIB scope authority is unfrozen (v0.4, pending human sign-off)"
    mitigation: "Proceed with current scope; flag if downstream artifacts propose expansion"
  - risk: "@react-pdf/renderer is preferred but unvalidated — no established PDF pattern in project"
    mitigation: "Isolate in services/reporting/shift-report/pdf/; template components are pure JSX, renderer-agnostic in data contract. Swap allowed without scope change per DEC-003."
  - risk: "Assembly service makes 7 source calls across 6 consumed contexts — latency budget is 3 seconds"
    mitigation: "Existing BFF endpoint already meets SLA; Promise.allSettled with partial degradation"
  - risk: "Cross-context change to EmailProvider interface"
    mitigation: "Optional attachments field is backward-compatible; existing callers unaffected"
  - risk: "DEC-003 (@react-pdf/renderer) is assumption-based — no codebase verification possible pre-build"
    mitigation: "WS3 implementation must validate renderer output early. WS5 includes PDF rendering verification test. If renderer proves unsuitable, substitute without scope change per DEC-003."
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: EXEC-065 — Shift Report

## Overview

The Shift Report assembles data from 6 consumed contexts (7 source calls) into a read-only `ShiftReportDTO`, renders it as an on-screen review surface and a canonical server-generated PDF, and supports CSV export (Section 1 only) and manual email distribution with PDF attachment.

This is assembly, canonical rendering, and bounded distribution work — not greenfield data layer construction. All data sources already exist and are API-served. No new database security model, RLS policy, SECURITY DEFINER pattern, or report-owned persistence is introduced (RULE-7). Bounded application-layer write-path logic is in scope only for manual report distribution through existing email infrastructure (`email_send_attempt` table, owned by EmailService).

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- ShiftReportDTO composite aggregate from 6 consumed contexts (7 source calls)
- On-screen report page at /admin/reports/shift-summary (review surface)
- Server-side canonical PDF generation (preferred renderer: @react-pdf/renderer, per DEC-003)
- CSV export of Section 1 per-table financial summary rows
- Manual email distribution of canonical PDF to bounded recipient classes (application-layer write path via existing EmailService)
- Backward-compatible extension of EmailProvider for PDF attachments

**Out of Scope:**
- Staff attribution / "Who acted?" section (requires audit query service)
- Scheduled or recurring email delivery
- Multi-template support or recipient-specific variants
- Historical trend / comparative reporting
- Custom time windows (casino-configured shift boundaries only)
- New database security model, RLS policies, SECURITY DEFINER patterns, or report-owned persistence

## Architecture Context

- **Reporting surface:** Application composition layer (read-only cross-context orchestration; no owned tables, no domain invariants, no SRM authority changes). This is not a schema-owning bounded context — it introduces no owned persistence.
- **Consumed contexts (6):** table-context, shift-intelligence, mtl, measurement, loyalty, email
- **Source calls (7):** getShiftDashboardSummary, getShiftCashObsTable, getAnomalyAlerts, getAlertQuality, queryGamingDaySummary, MeasurementService.getSummary, rpc_shift_active_visitors_summary
- **Pattern:** Pattern A Contract-First DTOs, functional service factory
- **Assembly pattern:** Promise.allSettled() parallel fetch (matches MeasurementService)
- **PDF renderer:** @react-pdf/renderer (preferred initial path per DEC-003, subject to WS3/WS5 validation)
- **Auth:** withServerAction middleware, casino_id from RLS context (ADR-024)

## Surface Classification (ADR-041)

| Field | Value | Justification |
|-------|-------|---------------|
| **Rendering Delivery** | RSC Prefetch + Hydration | Server component calls assembly service directly. 6 data sources above fold. Matches shift dashboard. |
| **Data Aggregation** | BFF Summary | Assembly service collapses 7 source calls across 6 contexts into one ShiftReportDTO server-side. |
| **Rejected: Client Shell** | Rejected | 7 source calls would require 7 HTTP round-trips, violating 3s SLA. |
| **Rejected: Client-side Fetch** | Rejected | Cross-context aggregation must happen server-side. |

**Metric Provenance:**
- MEAS-001: Theo Discrepancy (Derived Operational, request-time)
- MEAS-002: Audit Event Correlation (Compliance-Interpreted, request-time)
- MEAS-003: Rating Coverage (Derived Operational, request-time)
- MEAS-004: Loyalty Liability (Snapshot-Historical, periodic daily)
- MEAS-005–012: Shift Dashboard Metrics (Various, request-time)

All existing metrics — no new MEAS rows required.

## Workstream Details

### WS1: ShiftReportDTO & Assembly Service

**Purpose:** Define the composite ShiftReportDTO and create the assembly service that fetches data from 6 consumed contexts (7 source calls) in parallel and produces a display-ready DTO.

**Key Design Decisions:**
- Pattern A (Contract-First) — composite aggregate with cross-context assembly
- Functional factory: `createShiftReportService(supabase)` → `ShiftReportServiceInterface`
- Single method: `assembleShiftReport(params)` → `ShiftReportDTO`
- `Promise.allSettled()` for resilient parallel fetch (partial degradation on single-source failure)
- All business computation (totals reconciliation, coverage %, executive summary) occurs here
- Cash obs data inlined in financial summary (DEC-001)
- Theo discrepancy included as quality badge in Section 2 (DEC-002)

**Consumed Services:**
1. `getShiftDashboardSummary()` → Section 1, 5 (financial metrics, baseline quality)
2. `getShiftCashObsTable()` → Section 1 (telemetry-grade cash obs, inline)
3. `getAnomalyAlerts()` → Section 4 (anomaly alerts)
4. `getAlertQuality()` → Section 4 (alert quality metrics)
5. `queryGamingDaySummary()` → Section 3 (compliance MTL/CTR)
6. `MeasurementService.getSummary()` → Sections 2, 5, 6 (coverage, audit, loyalty, theo)
7. `rpc_shift_active_visitors_summary` → Section 2 (active visitors)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] ShiftReportDTO covers all report sections defined by the EXEC-065 template structure (derived from FIB E1)
- [ ] Assembly service uses Promise.allSettled() for resilient parallel fetch
- [ ] Partial failure produces degraded DTO (not crash)
- [ ] Financial totals reconcile (casino total = sum of table rows)
- [ ] Coverage % matches source computation
- [ ] No `as any` casts, no `console.*` in production
- [ ] Tests cover happy path, partial failure, empty data
- [ ] Coverage ≥ 80% for assembler.ts

### WS2: Report Page & Review Surface

**Purpose:** RSC page rendering the ShiftReportDTO as a structured document with date/shift selection, CSV export, and action toolbar.

**Component Hierarchy:**
```
page.tsx (RSC — calls assembly service directly)
  └── ShiftReportShell (client — 'use client')
        ├── [Selection mode] Date picker + Shift selector + "Generate" button
        └── [Report mode]
              ├── ReportActionToolbar (PDF, CSV, Send buttons — OUTSIDE document)
              └── ShiftReportDocument (document container — print-safe)
                    ├── ReportHeader
                    ├── ExecutiveSummary
                    ├── FinancialSummary (with CSV export hook)
                    ├── RatingCoverage (with theo discrepancy badge)
                    ├── ComplianceSummary
                    ├── Anomalies
                    ├── BaselineQuality
                    ├── LoyaltyLiability
                    └── ReportFooter
```

**Key Design Decisions:**
- RSC page fetches data server-side (no fetch() calls)
- URL params for date/shift state (shareable links, back button)
- Document-style rendering — NOT dashboard cards/grids
- PT-2 Visual DNA brutalist-industrial styling adapted for document context
- CSV export: client-side generation from DTO financial_summary rows
- Action buttons sit outside the document rendering area
- Derivative highlighting only (existing alert statuses, not new scoring)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Report page renders 9 sections in fixed order
- [ ] Date picker uses gaming day from casino settings (RULE-6)
- [ ] Shift selector shows only configured boundaries (swing/day/grave)
- [ ] CSV export produces columns: table_label, game_type, drop, fills, credits, win_loss, hold_pct
- [ ] No business logic in section components
- [ ] Loading skeleton matches document structure
- [ ] Action buttons are outside the document rendering area

### WS3: PDF Generation Route

**Purpose:** Server-side canonical PDF generation. Preferred initial renderer: @react-pdf/renderer (per DEC-003), subject to validation during implementation.

**Key Design Decisions:**
- Renderer: @react-pdf/renderer preferred (JSX templates, Node.js native, built-in pagination). Swap allowed without scope change if it fails operational requirements, per DEC-003.
- Route: POST /api/v1/reports/shift-summary/pdf
- Request: `{ gaming_day: string, shift_boundary: 'swing' | 'day' | 'grave' }`
- Response: PDF binary with Content-Type: application/pdf
- Template components parallel the on-screen sections (9 sections, fixed order)
- Controlled pagination: page breaks between major sections
- Header/footer on each page: casino name, gaming day, page N of M, reference ID

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Route returns Content-Type: application/pdf
- [ ] Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="shift-report-{casino}-{date}-{shift}.pdf"
- [ ] PDF contains 9 sections in fixed order
- [ ] Each page has header (casino name, gaming day) and footer (page N of M, reference ID)
- [ ] No business logic in template components
- [ ] Error returns proper JSON error response (not malformed PDF)

### WS4: Email Distribution Route

**Purpose:** Manual email distribution of canonical PDF to bounded recipient classes.

**Key Design Decisions:**
- Route: POST /api/v1/reports/shift-summary/send
- Request: `{ gaming_day, shift_boundary, recipients: string[] }`
- Response: ServiceHttpResult<EmailSendResult>
- Idempotency reuses existing email send-attempt tracking (verified: `services/email/index.ts:44` → `crud.insertSendAttempt` → `email_send_attempt` table)
- Cross-context change: extend EmailProvider.send() with optional attachments field
- PDF generated in-memory via WS3 renderer, attached to email
- Recipient validation: email format only (UI constrains to defined classes)

**Cross-Context Changes (backward-compatible):**
- `lib/email/types.ts` — add optional `attachments` to EmailProvider.send() input
- `lib/email/resend-adapter.ts` — thread attachments to resend.emails.send()
- `services/email/dtos.ts` — add optional `attachment` to ShiftReportEmailInput
- `services/email/index.ts` — thread attachment through sendShiftReport()

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Route returns ServiceHttpResult<EmailSendResult>
- [ ] Idempotency key enforced (400 without it)
- [ ] Zod validation rejects empty recipients, invalid emails, invalid shift_boundary
- [ ] Existing EmailService tests still pass (backward-compatible)
- [ ] No scheduling or automation logic present

### WS5: Integration Tests & Quality Gates

**Purpose:** Assembly service unit tests and route handler HTTP contract tests.

**Test Coverage:**
- `assembler.test.ts`: happy path, partial failure, empty data, totals reconciliation, cash obs inline, theo badge
- `http-contract.test.ts`: PDF route returns binary, send route returns ServiceHttpResult, idempotency enforcement, validation errors

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Coverage ≥ 80% for assembler.ts
- [ ] No regressions in existing service tests

### WS_E2E: E2E Write-Path Tests

**Purpose:** Playwright specs for the email send write-path journey.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] At least 1 spec file in e2e/reporting/
- [ ] Tests cover: navigate to report, select date/shift, generate, send
- [ ] All specs pass

## Definition of Done

- [ ] All workstream outputs created
- [ ] All gates pass (type-check, test-pass, build)
- [ ] E2E specs pass for write-path journey (email send)
- [ ] No regressions in existing tests
- [ ] PDF renderer dependency installed and validated (DEC-003)
- [ ] API Surface Catalogue and OpenAPI spec updated if reporting routes are part of the governed v1 route inventory
- [ ] Security: no new database security model, RLS, or SECURITY DEFINER changes (RULE-7)
- [ ] Cross-context email changes are backward-compatible
- [ ] Existing EmailService tests still pass
